member.name: T:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeApi
member.summary: Provides a base interface for Senzing native SDK's can have failures occur.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeApi.GetLastException
member.summary: Returns a string about the last error the system received.
member.remarks: This is most commonly called after an Senzing function returns a failure code (non-zero or NULL).
member.returns: An error message

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeApi.GetLastExceptionCode
member.summary: Returns the exception code of the last error the system received.
member.remarks: This is most commonly called after a Senzing function returns a failure code (non-zero or NULL).
member.returns: An error code

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeApi.ClearLastException
member.summary: Clears the information about the last error the system received.

member.name: T:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfig
member.summary: Defines the C# interface to the Senzing config functions.
member.remarks: The Senzing config functions provide information regarding the Senzing config installation and user license.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfig.Init(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the Senzing config API with the specified module name, init parameters and flag indicating verbose logging.
member.param: A short name given to this instance of the config API.
A JSON string containing configuration parameters.
Enable diagnostic logging which will print a massive amount of information to stdout.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfig.Destroy
member.summary: Uninitializes the Senzing config API.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfig.Create(System.IntPtr@)
member.summary: Creates a new in-memory configuration using the default template and sets the specified parameter with the value of the configuration handle for working with it.
member.param: The out parameter on which to set the value of the configuration handle.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfig.Load(System.String,System.IntPtr@)
member.summary: Creates a new in-memory configuration using the specified JSON text and sets the specified parameter with the value of the configuration handle for working with it.
member.param: The JSON text for the config.
The out parameter on which to set the value of the configuration handle.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfig.Export(System.IntPtr,System.String@)
member.summary: Sets the JSON text for the configuration associated with the specified configuration handle to the specified response out parameter.
member.param: The configuration handle to export the JSON text from.
The out parameter which will be set with the JSON configuration text.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfig.Close(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Closes the in-memory configuration associated with the specified config handle and cleans up system resources.
member.remarks: After calling this method, the configuration handle can no longer be used and becomes invalid.
member.param: The config handle identifying the in-memory configuration to close.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfig.GetDataSourceRegistry(System.IntPtr,System.String@)
member.summary: Extracts the data sources from the in-memory configuration associated with the specified config handle and sets the specified response out parameter to the JSON text describing the data sources from the configuration.
member.param: The config handle identifying the in-memory configuration from which to list the data sources.
The out parameter that will be set to the JSON string describing the data sources from the configuration.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfig.RegisterDataSource(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Adds a data source described by the specified JSON to the in-memory configuration associated with the specified config handle.
member.param: The config handle identifying the in-memory to which to add the data source.
The JSON text describing the data source to add to the configuration.
The out parameter to set to the JSON string describing the data source that was added.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfig.UnregisterDataSource(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary: Deletes the data source described by the specified JSON from the in-memory configuration associated with the specified config handle.
member.param: The config handle identifying the in-memory configuration from which to delete the data source.
The JSON text describing the data source to delete.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: T:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfigManager
member.summary: Defines the C# interface to the Senzing config management functions.

member.summary: Initializes the Senzing config management API with the specified module name, init parameters and flag indicating verbose logging.

Enable diagnostic logging which will print a massive amount of information to stdout.

The comments for the configuration.
The configuration ID for the registered config.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.summary: Gets the configuration with the specified config ID and sets the specified out parameter to the JSON text of configuration.

member.param: The out parameter to set to the JSON text of the configuration list.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfigManager.SetDefaultConfigID(System.Int64)
member.summary: Sets the default configuration for the repository to the specified configuration ID.
member.param: The configuration ID to set as the default configuration.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfigManager.GetDefaultConfigID(System.Int64@)
member.summary: Gets the configuration ID of the default configuration for the repository and sets the specified out parameter to the value of the configuration ID.
member.param: The out parameter to be set to the default default configuration ID.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeConfigManager.ReplaceDefaultConfigID(System.Int64,System.Int64)
member.summary: Replaces the current configuration ID of the repository with the specified new configuration ID providing the current configuration ID of the repository is equal to the specified old configuration ID.
member.remarks: If the current configuration ID is not the same as the specified old configuration ID then this method fails to replace the default configuration ID with the new value.
member.param: The configuration ID that is believed to be the current default configuration ID.
The new configuration ID for the repository.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.param: A JSON document containing the attribute information defining the criteria for the search.
The entity ID of the entity to compare against the search criteria.
The search-profile identifier identifying the search profile to use for the search operation.
The flags to control how the operation is performed and specifically the content of the response JSON document.
The string response out parameter that will be set to the JSON response document describing the search results.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeEngine.ExportJSONEntityReport(System.Int64,System.IntPtr@)
member.summary: Begins an export of entity data from the repository, returning an export-handle that can be used to fetch the data in single-entity chunks in JSON format.
member.remarks: The exported data is read via the function and should be closed via the function when the export is complete.
member.param: The flags to control how the operation is performed and specifically the content of the response JSON document.
The out IntPtr parameter that is set to the value of the export handle.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeEngine.ExportCSVEntityReport(System.String,System.Int64,System.IntPtr@)
member.summary: Begins an export of entity data from the repository, returning an export-handle that can be used to fetch the data in single entity rows in CSV format.
member.remarks:
The exported data is read via the function and should be closed via the function when the export is complete.
The first output row returned by the export-handle contains the column headers as a string. Each following row contains the exported entity data.
member.param: Specify "*" to indicate "all columns", specify empty-string to indicate the "standard columns", otherwise specify a comma-separated list of column names.
The flags to control how the operation is performed and specifically the content of the response JSON document.
The out IntPtr parameter that is set to the value of the export handle.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.param: The export handle for the export.
The out string response parameter to set to the JSON response document.
member.returns: Zero (0) on success and non-zero on failure.

member.name: T:Senzing.Sdk.Core.NativeProduct
member.summary: Defines the C# interface to the Senzing product functions.
member.remarks: The Senzing product functions provide information regarding the Senzing product installation and user license.

member.summary: Initializes the Senzing product API with the specified module name, init parameters and flag indicating verbose logging.
member.param: A short name given to this instance of the product API.
A JSON string containing configuration parameters.
Enable diagnostic logging which will print a massive amount of information to stdout.

member.returns: A JSON document describing version details.

member.summary: The default "bootstrap" settings with which to initialize the SzCoreEnvironment when an explicit settings value has not been provided via the 's Settings method.
member.remarks:
If this is used it will initialize Senzing for access to only the and interfaces when Senzing is installed in the default location for the platform. The value of this constant is "{ }".
NOTE: Using these settings is only useful for accessing the and interfaces since , and require database configuration to connect to the Senzing repository.

member.summary: The number of milliseconds to delay (if not notified) until checking if we are destroyed.

member.summary: Internal object for class-wide synchronized locking.

member.name: T:Senzing.Sdk.Core.SzCoreEnvironment.State
member.summary: Enumerates the possible states for an instance of SzCoreEnvironment.

member.name: F:Senzing.Sdk.Core.SzCoreEnvironment.State.Active
member.summary: If an SzCoreEnvironment instance is in the "active" state then it is initialized and ready to use.
member.remarks: Only one instance of SzCoreEnvironment can exist in the or state because Senzing environment cannot be initialized heterogeneously within a single process.

member.name: F:Senzing.Sdk.Core.SzCoreEnvironment.State.Destroying
member.summary: An instance SzCoreEnvironment moves to the "destroying" state when the method has been called but has not yet completed any Senzing operations that are already in-progress.
member.remarks: In this state, the SzCoreEnvironment will fast-fail any newly attempted operations with an , but will complete those Senzing operations that were initiated before was called.

member.name: F:Senzing.Sdk.Core.SzCoreEnvironment.State.Destroyed
member.summary: An instance of SzCoreEnvironment moves to the "destroyed" state when the method has completed and there are no more Senzing operations that are in-progress.
member.remarks: Once an instance moves to then a new active instance can be created and initialized.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.SzCoreEnvironment.NewBuilder
member.summary: Creates a new instance of for setting up an instance of SzCoreEnvironment.

remarks.para: Keep in mind that while multiple instances can exists, only one active instance of SzCoreEnvironment can exist at time. An active instance is one that has not yet been destroyed.
Alternatively, you can directly call the constructor.

member.returns: The for configuring and initializing the SzCoreEnvironment.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.SzCoreEnvironment.ValidateActiveInstance
member.summary: Internal method that is called by to validate the active instance before returning it.
member.remarks: It is expected that if this instance is in the process of destroying itself, then this method will block until the completion of the method. true is returned if this instance is active and has not had its method called, otherwise false is returned for an instance that was already destroyed.
member.para: This method should be overridden by derived classes that override .
IMPORTANT: If this instance is in the process of being destroyed (e.g.: waiting for in-flight operations to complete) then this method must block until destruction is complete.
member.returns: true if this instance if still active, or false if this instance has been destroyed.

member.summary: Attempts to acquire an exclusive write lock from this instance's if the lock is not already held.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.Core.SzCoreEnvironment.AcquireReadLock
member.summary: Attempts to acquire a shared read lock from this instance's if the lock is not already held.
member.returns: This returns the for this instance if the lock was not held and was obtained by this call, otherwise it returns null to indicate the lock was already held.

members.member:
Releases any locks held on the specified providing it is not null.
If the specified parameter is null then this method does nothing and simply returns null.
The to release the lock on, or null if no lock was actually obtained by the calling stack frame.
Always returns null so the calling stack frame can set its local variable to the result of this function on a single line. .

member.name: T:Senzing.Sdk.Core.SzCoreUtilities
member.summary: Provides utility functions that are useful when implementing the Senzing SDK interfaces from the Senzing.Sdk namespace. This is functionality leveraged by the "core" implementation that is made visible to aid in other implementations.

member.name: T:Senzing.Sdk.SzConfig
member.summary: Defines the C# interface that encapsulates and represents a Senzing configuration and provides functions to operate on that configuration.

remarks.para: The Senzing config functions provide means to create, manipulate and export Senzing JSON configurations.
An SzConfig instance is typically obtained from an instance via one of the following methods:

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzConfig.Export
member.summary: Retrieves the definition for this configuration.
member.remarks: NOTE: Typically, an implementations ToString() function will be implemented to return the result from this function.
member.example:
Example Result:
The example result is rather large, but can be viewed here (formatted for readability).
member.returns: The configuration definition formatted as a JSON object.
member.exception: If a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzConfig.GetDataSourceRegistry
member.summary: Gets the data source registry for this configuration.
member.returns: The data source registry describing the data sources for this configuration formatted as a JSON object.
member.exception: If a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzConfig.RegisterDataSource(System.String)
member.summary: Adds a data source to this configuration.

remarks.para: Because is an in-memory representation, the repository is not changed unless the configuration is exported and then registered via .
An exception is thrown if the data source already exists in the configuration.

member.param: The data source code for the new data source.
member.returns: The JSON string describing the data source was registered.
member.exception: If a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzConfig.UnregisterDataSource(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a data source from this configuration.

remarks.para: Because is an in-memory representation, the repository is not changed unless the configuration is exported and then registered via .
NOTE: This method is idempotent in that it succeeds with no changes being made when specifying a data source code that is not found in the registry.
WARNING: If records in the repository refer to the unregistered data source, the configuration cannot be used as the active configuration.

member.param: The data source code that identifies the data source to delete from the configuration.
member.exception: If a failure occurs.

Unless an explicit configuration ID is specified at initialization, the default configuration ID is used.
NOTE: The default configuration ID may not be the same as the active configuration ID.

member.summary: Replaces the existing default configuration ID with a new configuration ID.

member.exception: If the default configuration ID was not updated to the specified new value because the current default configuration ID found in the repository was not equal to the specified expected current default configuration ID value.

member.exception: If a failure occurs.

member.name: T:Senzing.Sdk.SzConfigRetryable
member.summary: Used to annotate which Senzing SDK methods are dependent upon the active configuration being current.

remarks.para: Methods annotated with this annotation are those methods that may experience a failure if the active configuration is not the most recent configuration for the repository. Such failures can occur when retrieved data references a configuration element (e.g.: data source, feature type or match type) that is not found in the active configuration.

There are various ways this can happen, but typically it means that data has been loaded through another using a newer configuration (usually in another system process) and then that data is retrieved by an that was initialized prior to that configuration being registered in the repository and being set as the default configuration.

Such failures can usually be resolved by retrying after checking if the active configuration ID differs from the current default configuration ID, and if so reinitializing using the current default configuration ID

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzConfigRetryable.#ctor
member.summary: Default constructor.

member.remarks: Typically, this is only run when troubleshooting performance. This is a non-destructive test.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzDiagnostic.PurgeRepository
member.summary: Permanently deletes all data in the repository, except the configuration.
member.remarks: WARNING: This method is destructive, it will delete all loaded records and entity resolution decisions. Senzing does not provide a means to the restore the data. The only means of recovery would be restoring from a database backup.
member.exception: Thrown if a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.AddRecord(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Nullable{Senzing.Sdk.SzFlag})
member.summary: Loads a record into the repository and performs entity resolution.

remarks.para: If a record already exists with the same data source code and record ID, it will be replaced. If the record definition contains DATA_SOURCE and RECORD_ID JSON keys, the values must match the dataSourceCode and recordId parameters.

The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values not only controls how the operation is performed, but also the content of the response. Any value may be included, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags to recognized flags).

member.param: The data source code identifying the data source for the record being added.
The record ID that uniquely identifies the record being added within the scope of its associated data source.
The string that defines the record, typically in JSON format.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response. Omitting this parameter will default its value to . Specify for an INFO response. Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The JSON string result produced by adding the record to the repository, or null if the specified flags do not indicate that an INFO message should be returned.
member.exception: If an unrecognized data source code is specified.
If the specified record definition has a data source or record ID value that conflicts with the specified data source code and/or record ID values.
If a failure occurs.
member.seealso: Code Snippet: Load Truth Set "With Info"
Code Snippet: Load "With Info" via Futures
Code Snippet: Load "With Stats" Via Loop

member.summary: Describes the features resulting from the hypothetical load of a record.

remarks.para: This method is used to preview the features for a record that has not been loaded.

The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values not only controls how the operation is performed, but also the content of the response. Any value may be included, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags to recognized flags).

member.param: The string that defines the record, typically in JSON format.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response. Omitting this parameter will default its value to . Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The JSON string result produced by getting a record preview (depending on the specified flags).
member.exception: If a failure occurs.

remarks.para: NOTE: This method is idempotent in that it succeeds with no changes being made when the record is not found in the repository.

The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values not only controls how the operation is performed, but also the content of the response. Any value may be included, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags to recognized flags).

member.param: The data source code identifying the data source for the record being deleted.
The record ID that uniquely identifies the record being deleted within the scope of its associated data source.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response. Omitting this parameter will default its value to . Specify for an INFO response. Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The JSON string result produced by deleting the record from the repository, or null if the specified flags do not indicate that an INFO message should be returned.
member.exception: If an unrecognized data source code is specified.
If a failure occurs.
member.seealso: Code Snippet: Delete "With Info" via Futures

The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values not only controls how the operation is performed, but also the content of the response. Any value may be included, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags to recognized flags).

The record ID that uniquely identifies the record to reevaluate within the scope of its associated data source.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response. Omitting this parameter will default its value to . Specify for an INFO response. Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.SearchByAttributes(System.String,System.String,System.Nullable{Senzing.Sdk.SzFlag})
member.summary: Searches for entities that match or relate to the provided attributes.

remarks.para: The default search profile is "SEARCH". Alternatively, "INGEST" may be used.
If the specified search profile is null then the default will be used (alternatively, use as a convenience method to omit the parameter).

The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values not only control how the search is performed but also the content of the response. Any value may be included, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags to recognized flags).

member.param: The search attributes defining the hypothetical record to match and/or relate to in order to obtain the search results.
The optional search profile identifier, or null if the default search profile should be used for the search.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response, omitting this parameter will default its value to for the default recommended flags. Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The resulting JSON string describing the result of the search.
member.exception: If a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.SearchByAttributes(System.String,System.Nullable{Senzing.Sdk.SzFlag})
member.summary:
Convenience method for calling
with a null value for the search profile parameter.
member.remarks: See documentation for details.
member.param: The search attributes defining the hypothetical record to match and/or relate to in order to obtain the search results.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response, omitting this parameter will default its value to for the default recommended flags. Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The resulting JSON string describing the result of the search.
member.exception: If a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.WhySearch(System.String,System.Int64,System.String,System.Nullable{Senzing.Sdk.SzFlag})
member.summary: Describes the ways a set of search attributes relate to an entity.
member.remarks:
The default search profile is "SEARCH". Alternatively, "INGEST" may be used.
If the specified search profile is null then the default will be used.
The specified search attributes are treated as a hypothetical single-record entity and the result of this operation is the "why analysis" of the entity identified by the specified entity ID against that hypothetical entity. The details included in the response are determined by the specified flags.
If the specified search profile is null then the default generic thresholds from the default search profile will be used for the search candidate determination. If your search requires different behavior using alternate generic thresholds, please contact support@senzing.com for details on configuring a custom search profile.
The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values not only control how the search is performed but also the content of the response. Any value may be included, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags to recognized flags).
member.param: The search attributes defining the hypothetical record to match and/or relate to in order to obtain the search results.
The entity ID identifying the entity to analyze against the search attribute criteria.
The optional search profile identifier, or null if the default search profile should be used for the search.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response, omitting this parameter will default its value to for the default recommended flags. Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The resulting JSON string describing the result of the why analysis against the search criteria.
member.exception: If no entity could be found with the specified entity ID.
If a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.FindPath(System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.ISet{System.Int64},System.Collections.Generic.ISet{System.String},System.Nullable{Senzing.Sdk.SzFlag})
member.summary: Searches for the shortest relationship path between two entities, specified by entity IDs.

remarks.para: The returned path is the shortest path among the paths that satisfy the parameters.

The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values not only control how the operation is performed but also the level of detail provided for the entity path and those entities on the path. Any value may be included, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags to recognized flags).

member.param: The entity ID of the first entity.
The entity ID of the second entity.
The maximum number of degrees for the path search.

The optional of long entity ID's identifying entities to be avoided when finding the path, or null if no entities are to be avoided. By default the entities will be avoided unless necessary to find the path. To strictly avoid the entities specify the flag.
The optional of non-null string data source codes identifying the data sources for which at least one record must be included on the path, or null if none are required.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response. Omitting this parameter will default its value to the default recommended flags (). Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The JSON string describing the resultant entity path which may be an empty path if no path exists between the two entities given the path parameters.
member.exception: If either the path-start or path-end entities for the specified entity ID's cannot be found.
If an unrecognized required data source is specified.
If a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.FindPath(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.ISet{System.ValueTuple{System.String,System.String}},System.Collections.Generic.ISet{System.String},System.Nullable{Senzing.Sdk.SzFlag})
member.summary: Searches for the shortest relationship path between two entities, specified by record IDs.

remarks.para: The returned path is the shortest path among the paths that satisfy the parameters.
The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values may contain any value, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags).

member.param: The data source code identifying the data source for the starting record of the requested path.
The record ID that uniquely identifies the starting record of the requested path within the scope of its associated data source.
The data source code identifying the data source for the ending record of the requested path.
The record ID that uniquely identifies the ending record of the requested path within the scope of its associated data source.
The maximum number of degrees for the path search.

The optional of tuples containing data source code and record ID pairs identifying records whose entities are to be avoided when finding the path, or null if no entities are to be avoided. By default the entities will be avoided unless necessary to find the path. To strictly avoid the entities specify the flag.
The optional of non-null string data source codes identifying the data sources for which at least one record must be included on the path, or null if none are required.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response. Omitting this parameter will default its value to the default recommended flags (). Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The JSON string describing the resultant entity path which may be an empty path if no path exists between the two entities given the path parameters.
member.exception: If either the path-start or path-end records for the specified data source code and record ID pairs cannot be found.
If an unrecognized data source code is specified.
If a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.FindNetwork(System.Collections.Generic.ISet{System.Int64},System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Nullable{Senzing.Sdk.SzFlag})
member.summary: Retrieves a network of relationships among entities, specified by entity IDs.

remarks.para: WARNING: Entity networks may be very large due to the volume of inter-related data in the repository. The parameters of this method can be used to limit the information returned.

The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values not only control how the operation is performed but also the content of the response. Any value may be included, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags to recognized flags).

member.param: The non-null of long entity ID's identifying the entities for which to build the network.

The maximum number of degrees for the path search between the specified entities. The maximum degrees of separation for the paths between entities must be specified so as to prevent the network growing beyond the desired size.

The number of relationship degrees to build out from each of the found entities on the network, or zero to prevent network build-out. If this is non-zero, the size of the network can be limited to a maximum total number of build-out entities for the whole network.
The maximum number of entities to build out for the entire network. This limits the size of the build-out network when the build-out degrees is non-zero.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response. Omitting this parameter will default its value to the default recommended flags (). Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The JSON string describing the resultant entity network and the entities on the network.
member.exception: If any of the entities for the specified entity ID's cannot be found.
If a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.FindNetwork(System.Collections.Generic.ISet{System.ValueTuple{System.String,System.String}},System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Nullable{Senzing.Sdk.SzFlag})
member.summary: Retrieves a network of relationships among entities, specified by record IDs.

remarks.para: WARNING: Entity networks may be very large due to the volume of inter-related data in the repository. The parameters of this method can be used to limit the information returned.

The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values not only control how the operation is performed but also the content of the response. Any value may be included, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags to recognized flags).

member.param: The non-null of tuples of data source code and record ID pairs identifying the constituent records of the entities for which to build the network.

The maximum number of degrees for the path search between the specified entities. The maximum degrees of separation for the paths between entities must be specified so as to prevent the network growing beyond the desired size.

The number of relationship degrees to build out from each of the found entities on the network, or zero to prevent network build-out. If this is non-zero, the size of the network can be limited to a maximum total number of build-out entities for the whole network.
The maximum number of entities to build out for the entire network. This limits the size of the build-out network when the build-out degrees is non-zero.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response. Omitting this parameter will default its value to the default recommended flags (). Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The JSON string describing the resultant entity network and the entities on the network.
member.exception: If an unrecognized data source code is specified.
If any of the records for the specified data source code and record ID pairs cannot be found.
If a failure occurs.

member.param: Specify "*" to indicate "all columns", specify empty-string to indicate the "standard columns", otherwise specify a comma-separated list of column names.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response. Omitting this parameter defaults it value to for the default recommended flags. Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The export handle to use for retrieving the export data.
member.exception: If a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.FetchNext(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Fetches the next line of entity data from an open export report.

remarks.para: Used in conjunction with , and .
If the export handle was obtained from , this returns the CSV header on the first call and exported entity data in CSV format on subsequent calls.
If the export handle was obtained from this returns exported entity data as a JSON object.
When null is returned, the export report is complete and the caller should invoke to free resources.

example.code: {"RESOLVED_ENTITY":{"ENTITY_ID":1,"ENTITY_NAME":"Joseph Schmidt","FEATURES":{"ADDRESS":[{"FEAT_DESC":"101 Main Street, Los Angeles, CA 90011","LIB_FEAT_ID":3,"FEAT_DESC_VALUES":[{"FEAT_DESC":"101 Main Street, Los Angeles, CA 90011","LIB_FEAT_ID":3}]}],"DOB":[{"FEAT_DESC":"12-JAN-1981","LIB_FEAT_ID":2,"FEAT_DESC_VALUES":[{"FEAT_DESC":"12-JAN-1981","LIB_FEAT_ID":2}]}],"NAME":[{"FEAT_DESC":"Joseph Schmidt","LIB_FEAT_ID":1,"FEAT_DESC_VALUES":[{"FEAT_DESC":"Joseph Schmidt","LIB_FEAT_ID":1}]}],"PHONE":[{"FEAT_DESC":"818-777-2424","LIB_FEAT_ID":4,"USAGE_TYPE":"HOME","FEAT_DESC_VALUES":[{"FEAT_DESC":"818-777-2424","LIB_FEAT_ID":4}]},{"FEAT_DESC":"213-555-1212","LIB_FEAT_ID":5,"USAGE_TYPE":"MOBILE","FEAT_DESC_VALUES":[{"FEAT_DESC":"213-555-1212","LIB_FEAT_ID":5}]}]},"RECORD_SUMMARY":[{"DATA_SOURCE":"PASSENGERS","RECORD_COUNT":1}],"RECORDS":[{"DATA_SOURCE":"PASSENGERS","RECORD_ID":"ABC123","INTERNAL_ID":1,"MATCH_KEY":"","MATCH_LEVEL_CODE":"","ERRULE_CODE":""}]},"RELATED_ENTITIES":[{"ENTITY_ID":2,"MATCH_LEVEL_CODE":"POSSIBLY_RELATED","MATCH_KEY":"+PHONE-DOB","ERRULE_CODE":"SF1","IS_DISCLOSED":0,"IS_AMBIGUOUS":0,"ENTITY_NAME":"Joann Smith","RECORD_SUMMARY":[{"DATA_SOURCE":"PASSENGERS","RECORD_COUNT":1}]},{"ENTITY_ID":5,"MATCH_LEVEL_CODE":"POSSIBLY_RELATED","MATCH_KEY":"+ADDRESS-DOB","ERRULE_CODE":"SFF","IS_DISCLOSED":0,"IS_AMBIGUOUS":0,"ENTITY_NAME":"Bill Bandley","RECORD_SUMMARY":[{"DATA_SOURCE":"EMPLOYEES","RECORD_COUNT":1}]}]}

RESOLVED_ENTITY_ID,RESOLVED_ENTITY_NAME,RELATED_ENTITY_ID,MATCH_LEVEL,MATCH_LEVEL_CODE,MATCH_KEY,MATCH_KEY_DETAILS,IS_DISCLOSED,IS_AMBIGUOUS,DATA_SOURCE,RECORD_ID,JSON_DATA,FIRST_SEEN_DT,LAST_SEEN_DT,UNMAPPED_DATA,ERRULE_CODE,RELATED_ENTITY_NAME

member.param: The export handle for the export report from which to retrieve the next line of data.
member.returns: The next line of export data whose format depends on which function was used to initiate the export, or null if there is no more data to be exported via the specified handle.
member.exception: If the specified export handle has already been closed or if any other failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.CloseExportReport(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Closes an export report.
member.remarks: Used in conjunction with , and .
member.param: The export handle of the export report to close.
member.exception: If the specified export handle has already been closed or if any other failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.ProcessRedoRecord(System.String,System.Nullable{Senzing.Sdk.SzFlag})
member.summary: Processes the provided redo record.

remarks.para: This operation performs entity resolution. Calling this method has the potential to create more redo records in certain situations.
This method is used in conjunction with and .

The optionally specified bitwise-OR'd values not only controls how the operation is performed, but also the content of the response. Any value may be included, but only flags belonging to the group will be recognized (other values will be ignored unless they have equivalent bit flags to recognized flags).

member.param: The redo record to be processed.

The optional bitwise-OR'd values belonging to the group to control how the operation is performed and the content of the response. Omitting this parameter will default its value to . Specify for an INFO response. Specifying null is equivalent to specifying .
member.returns: The JSON string result produced by processing the redo record, or null if the specified flags do not indicate that an INFO message should be returned.
member.exception: If a failure occurs.
member.seealso: Code Snippet: Processing Redos while Loading
Code Snippet: Continuous Redo Processing via Futures
Code Snippet: Continuous Redo "With Info" Processing

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.GetRedoRecord
member.summary: Retrieves and removes a pending redo record.

remarks.para: A null value will be returned if there are no pending redo records. Use to process the result of this method. Once Once a redo record is retrieved, it is no longer tracked by Senzing. The redo record may be stored externally for later processing.
This method is used in conjunction with and .

member.returns: The retrieved redo record or null if there were no pending redo records.
member.exception: If a failure occurs.
member.seealso: Code Snippet: Processing Redos while Loading
Code Snippet: Continuous Redo Processing via Futures
Code Snippet: Continuous Redo "With Info" Processing

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzEngine.CountRedoRecords
member.summary: Gets the number of redo records pending processing.

remarks.para: WARNING: When there is a large number of redo records, this is an expensive call. Hint: If processing redo records, use result of to manage looping.
This method is used in conjunction with and .

member.returns: The number of redo records pending to be processed.
member.exception: If a failure occurs.
member.seealso: Code Snippet: Processing Redos while Loading
Code Snippet: Continuous Redo Processing via Futures
Code Snippet: Continuous Redo "With Info" Processing

members.member:
Enumerates the Senzing flag values so they can be referred to as bitwise enumerated flags.
Each SzFlag belongs to one or more instances which can be obtained via the extension method. This helps in identifying which flags are applicable to which functions since a function will document which to refer to for applicable flags.
Passing an SzFlag to a function to which it does not apply will either have no effect or activate an applicable that happens to have the same bitwise value as the non-applicable
See also:

The bitwise flag that indicates that the Senzing engine should produce and return the INFO document describing the affected entities from an operation.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzFlags.GetFlags(Senzing.Sdk.SzFlagUsageGroup)
member.summary: Obtains the flags belonging to an individual as an aggregate value for all flags associated with the specified group.
member.param: The individual for which to obtain the list of flags with their names.
member.returns: The aggregate value with the bits set for all flags belonging to specified individual .
member.exception: If the specified is itself an aggregate value of multiple groups or equal to (i.e.: zero).

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzFlags.GetNamesByFlag(Senzing.Sdk.SzFlagUsageGroup)
member.summary: Obtains the flags belonging to an individual as a read-only dictionary of individual keys mapped to symbolic name values.
member.remarks: NOTE: The is provided as an alternative to for disambiguation of different flags that otherwise have the same value (e.g.: and ) so the actual flag name can be obtained with respect to a specific since there is no ambiguity within a group.
member.param: The individual for which to obtain the list of flags with their names.
member.returns: A read-only dictionary of individual keys mapped to symbolic name values for those flags belonging to specified individual .
member.exception: If the specified is itself an aggregate value of multiple groups or equal to (i.e.: zero).

member.summary: Obtains the flags belonging to an individual as a read-only dictionary of individual symbolic name keys mapped to values.

member.param: The individual for which to obtain the list of flags with their names.
member.returns: A read-only dictionary of individual symbolic name keys mapped to values for those flags belonging to specified individual .
member.exception: If the specified is itself an aggregate value of multiple groups or equal to (i.e.: zero).

member.remarks: NOTE: The is provided for disambiguation of different flags that otherwise have the same value (e.g.: and ).
member.returns: The read-only dictionary of individual symbolic name keys mapped to values for all individual flag constants from the enum.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzFlags.FlagsToString(Senzing.Sdk.SzFlagUsageGroup,System.Nullable{Senzing.Sdk.SzFlag})
member.summary: Formats a string representation of the specified according to the instances associated with the respective .

remarks.para: NOTE: This method is useful in logging which flags were past to a particular method using the for the flags that are accepted by that method.

Some values have the same underlying bitwise flag value which can lead to ambiguity with the default ToString() implementation for enums. However, none of the instances for a single should overlap in bitwise values and this method will prefer the symbol belonging to the respective for formatting the string.

If the respective is an aggregate group value representing multiple groups (or no groups) then this method simply defaults to formatting the set bit values using numeric representation, foregoing the use of symbolic flag names altogether.

member.param: The on which to operate.
The value (which may be an aggregate value) to format as a string.
member.returns: The string describing the specified flags.

member.name: F:Senzing.Sdk.SzFlagUsageGroupSets.SzRelationSet
member.summary: The internal aggregate constant to apply to instances that retrieve entity data pertaining to inclusion of related entities and the details of related entities.

member.name: F:Senzing.Sdk.SzFlagUsageGroupSets.SzEntitySet
member.summary: The internal aggregate constant to apply to apply to instances that retrieve entity data since they are used by most operations.
member.remarks: Flags that use this aggregate constant should not affect inclusion of or details of related entities.

member.name: F:Senzing.Sdk.SzFlagUsageGroupSets.SzEntityHowSet
member.summary: The internal aggregate constant to apply to instances that retrieve entity data and also apply to "how" operations.
member.remarks: Flags that use this aggregate constant should not affect inclusion of or details of related entities.

member.name: F:Senzing.Sdk.SzFlagUsageGroupSets.SzEntityRecordSet
member.summary: The internal aggregate constant to apply to instances that retrieve record data since they are used by most other groups and can be specifically used for retrieving a single record.

member.name: F:Senzing.Sdk.SzFlagUsageGroupSets.SzRecordPreviewSet
member.summary: The internal aggregate constant to apply to instances that are applicable to getting a record preview. These are subset of those records applicable to retrieving record data and therefore an be used by any group that can retrieve entity data.

member.name: F:Senzing.Sdk.SzFlagUsageGroupSets.SzWhySearchSet
member.summary: The internal aggregate constant to apply to instances that can be used only for "why search" and "search" operations.

member.name: T:Senzing.Sdk.SzProduct

member.remarks: The Senzing product functions provide information regarding the Senzing product installation and user license.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzProduct.GetLicense
member.summary: Gets the details and entitlements of the applied product license.
member.remarks: NOTE: The details do not include the license key.
member.example:
Example Result: (formatted for readability)
{ "customer": "", "contract": "", "issueDate": "2025-10-06", "licenseType": "EVAL (Solely for non-productive use)", "licenseLevel": "", "billing": "", "expireDate": "2026-10-07", "recordLimit": 500, "advSearch": 0 }
member.returns: The JSON document describing the license details.
member.exception: Thrown if a failure occurs.

member.name: M:Senzing.Sdk.SzProduct.GetVersion
member.summary: Gets the product version details.
member.returns: The JSON document of version details.
member.exception: Thrown if a failure occurs.
